headline_summary: Iran says Strait of Hormuz is closed; IRGC warns it will fire on any ship attempting transit
Qatar shuts gas liquefaction and declares force majeure on LNG exports; restart expected to take weeks
Maritime war-risk insurance premiums surge (some >1000%) as Gulf conflict widens and Hormuz traffic is paralyzed
Hezbollah launches missiles/drones at Israel; Israel sends troops into south Lebanon and strikes Beirut
Katyusha rockets target US embassy in Baghdad, security sources say
risk_drivers: Explicit Strait of Hormuz closure threat / de facto traffic paralysis
Attacks on tankers, ports, and energy infrastructure across Gulf
LNG supply shock from Qatar force majeure
Multi-theatre escalation (Lebanon/Israel; Iraq attacks on US facilities)
Surging maritime insurance and cyber-retaliation risk

Energy: Higher crude risk premium; backwardation/volatility risk if Hormuz disruption persists.
LNG/European gas upside risk due to Qatari supply outage and constrained rerouting.
FX: EM oil-importer FX downside risk; USD/JPY and CHF bid on risk-off; Gulf pegs watched for reserve drawdowns.
Rates: Inflation shock risk lifts breakevens; front-end cuts repriced; credit spreads can widen on risk-off.
Equities: Risk-off tilt: energy/defense outperform; airlines/chemicals/transport underperform; higher vol.

sources.title: Iran vows to attack any ship trying to pass through Strait of Hormuz
Exclusive: Qatar shuts gas liquefaction, will take weeks to restart, sources say
Maritime insurance premiums surge as Iran conflict widens
Lebanon sucked deeper into war as Hezbollah, Israel trade blows
Katyusha rockets target US embassy in Baghdad, security sources say

events.title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz is closed; IRGC warns it will fire on any ship attempting transit
events.implication: Explicit closure threat raises immediate disruption risk for ~20% of global oil flows and elevates risk premia across energy and risk assets.

events.title: Maersk pauses Bab el-Mandeb/Suez transits and suspends Strait of Hormuz crossings amid escalation
events.implication: Container line rerouting signals broader trade/logistics shock and compounds Middle East maritime risk beyond crude flows.

events.title: Three tankers damaged near Strait of Hormuz; one seafarer killed as risks to shipping surge
events.location_name: Strait of Hormuz, near United Arab Emirates
events.implication: Multiple vessel damage incidents intensify war-risk repricing and can restrict effective oil/LNG tanker availability.

events.title: Projectile hits product tanker MKD VYOM off Muscat; crew member killed
events.implication: Fatal strike reinforces insurer/shipowner avoidance behavior and increases freight rates for Gulf-adjacent routes.

events.title: Maritime war-risk insurance premiums surge (some >1000%) as Gulf conflict widens and Hormuz traffic is paralyzed
events.implication: Insurance and security costs can become a de facto supply constraint, sustaining higher delivered energy prices and inflation pressure.

events.title: Drone attack hits fuel tank at Duqm port
events.implication: Port/energy infrastructure attacks broaden the conflict footprint and increase downside risks to regional logistics and refined products.

events.title: Qatar shuts gas liquefaction and declares force majeure on LNG exports; restart expected to take weeks
events.implication: Loss of ~20% global LNG supply risks European/Asian gas price spikes and raises broader inflation/industrial risk.

events.title: Hezbollah launches missiles/drones at Israel; Israel sends troops into south Lebanon and strikes Beirut
events.implication: Northern front escalation raises multi-theatre tail risk and complicates de-escalation pathways; supports risk-off and regional asset volatility.

events.title: Beirut hotel strike kills four; Israel says it targeted Iranian commanders
events.implication: Targeted strikes in Beirut increase probability of Hezbollah retaliation and wider disruption risk.

events.title: Katyusha rockets target US embassy in Baghdad, security sources say
events.implication: Attacks on US facilities in Iraq raise risk of US retaliation and additional sanctions/force deployments, reinforcing risk-off.

events.title: Hackers hit Iranian apps and websites alongside strikes; connectivity drops reported
events.implication: Cyber operations increase escalation pathways and raise operational risk for regional/Western firms, including potential spillover to finance/energy.

events.title: US banks put on high alert for potential Iranian cyberattacks as war escalates
events.implication: Elevated cyber risk can tighten financial conditions and amplify equity volatility if disruptions occur.

events.title: Putin offers to use Russia ties to Iran to help restore calm; calls with Gulf leaders emphasize ceasefire
events.implication: Diplomatic engagement may cap worst-case outcomes, but also signals conflict seen as high-risk by major stakeholders.

events.title: China condemns attacks on Iran and urges immediate ceasefire and talks
events.implication: Major-power calls for ceasefire underline systemic energy/trade risk; potential for UN/security council activity.

events.title: Turkey says it's engaging with all sides to end Iran war and resume diplomacy
events.implication: Regional mediation attempts suggest off-ramps, but ongoing strikes keep risk elevated.

events.title: Oil prices settle up around 5% on supply concerns as Iran conflict widens
events.implication: Energy price spike feeds inflation expectations and can pressure EM FX and widen credit spreads.

links.from: Iran says Strait of Hormuz is closed; IRGC warns it will fire on any ship attempting transit
links.why: Hormuz closure threatens a large share of seaborne oil exports, lifting crude risk premium.

links.from: Qatar shuts gas liquefaction and declares force majeure on LNG exports; restart expected to take weeks
links.why: Qatari LNG outage tightens global gas and can spill over into oil product substitution and broader energy risk premia.
Higher energy import bills and inflation typically weaken EM FX, especially net oil importers.
Energy-driven inflation shock can push yields/breakevens up and widen credit spreads on growth concerns.

links.from: Hezbollah launches missiles/drones at Israel; Israel sends troops into south Lebanon and strikes Beirut
links.why: Second-front escalation increases tail risk and prompts de-risking across global portfolios.

links.from: Katyusha rockets target US embassy in Baghdad, security sources say
links.why: Attacks on US facilities raise retaliation risk and uncertainty, typically triggering risk-off positioning.

links.from: Maritime war-risk insurance premiums surge (some >1000%) as Gulf conflict widens and Hormuz traffic is paralyzed
links.why: War-risk premium increases shipping costs and can reduce effective tanker supply, sustaining high delivered oil prices.
